title: Trial balance
summary: 9706 P2 — preparing a trial balance, suspense account, and errors revealed/not revealed.
learningObjectives: Prepare a trial balance from ledger balances.
Explain purpose and limitations of a trial balance.
Use a suspense account for temporary imbalance.
Identify errors not revealed by a balanced TB.

simpleExplanation.title: Checking double entry
simpleExplanation.summary: A trial balance lists all ledger balances; total debits should equal total credits. A balanced TB does not guarantee error-free accounts — some errors leave it balanced.
steps: Extract DR balances for assets and expenses; CR for liabilities and income.
Balanced TB — no arithmetical imbalance in double entry.
Unbalanced — post difference to suspense account temporarily.
Omission, commission, compensating errors may not show.

flashcards.back: Check arithmetic accuracy of double entry before final accounts.
Temporary account holding TB difference until errors found.
Omission, commission, principle (if both sides wrong class), compensating, original entry reversal if equal DR/CR.
Single-sided entry, unequal amounts, some transposition if one side wrong.
Suspense balance must be cleared to zero.

sections.content: **Trial balance** (1.4.2) — list **what TB can and cannot reveal** — frequent MCQ and explain question.

sections.question: Trial balance totals: DR $51,250, CR $51,000. A payment of $250 to a supplier was credited to cash but not debited to the supplier. Explain the imbalance and correction.
sections.solution:
**Credit side exceeds debit by $250** — classic **omitted debit** (cash CR recorded, payable DR missing).

**Suspense:** DR **Suspense** $250 to balance TB temporarily.

**Correction:** DR **Trade payable** $250, CR **Suspense** $250.

**Note:** Omission **does not** show as wrong individual balances until corrected — TB would still balance if both sides omitted (not the case here).
